title: Network Hardening and Monitoring
obj: Students will be able to apply network hardening techniques including disabling unused protocols, enabling logging, and using monitoring tools to detect anomalies.
content: Disable unused services and ports
close listening ports; disable Telnet, FTP, SNMPv1; use SSH instead of Telnet
Network segmentation
VLANs to isolate traffic; DMZ for public-facing servers; micro-segmentation concept
Firewall rules review
default-deny stance; removing overly permissive rules; logging denied traffic
IDS/IPS
intrusion detection vs. prevention; signature-based vs. anomaly-based; placement in network
Log monitoring
syslog, Windows Event Forwarding, SIEM basics; what to look for in network logs
Vulnerability scanning
Nessus, OpenVAS basics; agent vs. agentless; interpreting a basic scan report
assess: Hardening checklist: apply a provided network hardening checklist to a simulated environment
Log analysis: given a syslog excerpt, identify two anomalous events and explain why they are suspicious
Short answer: what is the difference between an IDS and an IPS?

title: Troubleshooting Motherboards, RAM, CPUs, and Power
obj: Students will be able to diagnose and resolve hardware failures in motherboards, RAM, CPUs, and power supplies using systematic techniques and diagnostic tools.
content: POST beep codes and LED indicators
interpreting BIOS beep codes (1 beep = OK, patterns = specific failures); diagnostic LEDs on modern boards
No POST troubleshooting
no power vs. powers on but no display; breadboarding technique; minimum hardware boot
RAM failures
blue screens with MEMORY_MANAGEMENT, random reboots; MemTest86; reseating, slot testing
CPU issues
overheating (thermal throttle → shutdown), bent pins (AMD), improper seating, cooler failure
Power supply testing
PSU tester, multimeter voltages (+12V, +5V, +3.3V rails); paperclip test; swapping with known-good
Motherboard failures
capacitor bulge, CMOS battery dead (time resets), POST checkpoint cards
assess: Symptom-to-cause matching: given 8 hardware failure symptoms, identify the most likely component
Lab: deliberately unseat a RAM stick, power on, observe the failure, then reseat and verify
Short answer: a PC powers on (fans spin, lights on) but shows no display — list three components to check in order

title: Troubleshooting Storage and RAID
obj: Students will be able to diagnose storage failures using SMART data, event logs, and diagnostic tools, and recover data or rebuild a RAID array.
content: HDD failure signs
clicking/grinding sounds, slow reads, SMART errors; CrystalDiskInfo health status
SSD failure signs
sudden read-only mode, corruption errors, disappearing drive, high reallocated sectors
SMART data interpretation
key attributes: Reallocated Sectors, Pending Sectors, Uncorrectable Errors; threshold vs. value
File system errors
chkdsk /f /r; NTFS journal corruption; dirty bit; when chkdsk runs at boot
RAID failure and rebuild
degraded array; replacing a failed drive; rebuild time and risk of second failure; importance of hot spare
Data recovery basics
when to attempt recovery (Recuva) vs. when to escalate to professional services; acting quickly matters
assess: SMART analysis: given a CrystalDiskInfo screenshot, classify the drive health and recommend action
chkdsk lab: run chkdsk on a volume with simulated errors and document the output
Short answer: why is a RAID 5 array at higher risk of total failure while one drive is rebuilding?

title: Troubleshooting Display Issues
obj: Students will be able to diagnose and resolve common display problems including no signal, artifacts, dead pixels, and backlight failures.
content: No signal
check cable seating, correct input source selected, test with different cable and monitor
Flickering and refresh rate
driver issue vs. cable bandwidth limitation vs. wrong refresh rate in settings
Artifacts and corruption
GPU overheating (check fan/thermal paste), VRAM failure, driver corruption
Dead and stuck pixels
dead (always black) vs. stuck (wrong color); pixel-fixing tools; manufacturer warranty thresholds
Backlight failure
dim image visible with flashlight = backlight dead not GPU; inverter vs. LED strip failure
Laptop display issues
loose hinge damaging cable, digitizer vs. display failure, external monitor as diagnostic tool
assess: Symptom diagnosis: given 6 display complaints, identify the most likely cause for each
Lab: use display test patterns to identify artifacts and document findings
Short answer: a user sees a faint image on their laptop screen — what component has likely failed?

title: Troubleshooting Mobile Device Issues
obj: Students will be able to diagnose and resolve common physical and connectivity issues on smartphones and tablets.
content: Battery issues
rapid drain (background apps, screen brightness), swollen battery (safety hazard), won't charge (cable/port/charge IC)
Screen and touch issues
cracked digitizer, touch not registering, ghost touches, liquid damage indicators
Connectivity problems
Wi-Fi dropping (forget/rejoin, reset network settings), Bluetooth won't pair, cellular no service
Physical damage
water damage steps (power off immediately, do not charge, desiccant); dropped-damage triage
Performance issues
clearing app cache, factory reset as last resort, storage full causing slowdown
Update and boot issues
stuck on boot logo (soft reset, recovery mode), update failure (free up storage, stable Wi-Fi required)
assess: Scenario triage: given 6 mobile complaints, list the first two diagnostic steps for each
Lab: on a practice device, demonstrate clearing app cache, checking battery health, and resetting network settings
Short answer: a user's phone is hot and the battery drains in 2 hours — list three likely causes

title: Troubleshooting Printer Issues
obj: Students will be able to diagnose and resolve common printer problems including paper jams, print quality defects, and connectivity failures.
content: Paper jams
locating jam (all access panels), removing paper fully without tearing, roller wear as root cause
Print quality issues
faded print (low toner/ink), streaks (dirty drum/cartridge), smearing (fuser failure), wrong colors
Connectivity issues
offline status, wrong IP, spooler stuck, driver mismatch between 32/64-bit
Laser-specific problems
background ghosting (drum worn), lines (drum scratch), toner not fusing (fuser temperature)
Inkjet-specific problems
clogged nozzles (print-head cleaning), ink smearing (wrong paper type), banding
Network printer issues
discovering printer by IP, checking print queue on server, clearing stuck jobs via spooler
assess: Print defect diagnosis: given 6 sample printout images, identify the cause of each defect
Spooler fix lab: simulate a stuck print job and clear it via the Services console and print queue
Short answer: a laser printer's output smears when touched immediately after printing — which component is failing?

title: Troubleshooting Windows OS
obj: Students will be able to diagnose and resolve common Windows OS issues including boot failures, BSODs, application errors, and performance problems.
content: Boot failure types
no bootable device (MBR/BCD corrupt), BOOTMGR missing, boot loop; WinRE access (F8/Shift+F8)
Windows Recovery Environment
Startup Repair, System Restore, System Image Recovery, Command Prompt, Reset This PC
BSOD analysis
reading stop codes, minidump files, WinDbg basics; common codes (IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL, PAGE_FAULT)
Application errors
application won't start, crashes on launch; event viewer application log; compatibility mode; reinstall
Performance troubleshooting
Task Manager bottleneck identification; disk cleanup; disabling startup items; SFC and DISM
User profile corruption
can't log in, temp profile, fixing with regedit ProfileList or creating new profile
assess: Boot repair lab: break a VM's BCD and repair it using bootrec /fixbcd and /fixmbr from WinRE
BSOD exercise: given a minidump analysis output, identify the failing driver
Short answer: what is the difference between SFC /scannow and DISM /RestoreHealth?

title: Troubleshooting Mobile OS and Apps
obj: Students will be able to diagnose and resolve software issues on iOS and Android devices including app crashes, update failures, and account sync problems.
content: App crashes
force-close, clear cache/data, uninstall/reinstall, check for update, check OS compatibility
OS update failures
insufficient storage, failed download (retry on Wi-Fi), update loop (Recovery Mode)
Account sync issues
sign out and back in, check network, reset sync, re-add account
Accessibility and display problems
zoom stuck, inverted colors, screen rotation locked; where to find each setting
Factory reset procedures
Android factory reset steps (Settings and Recovery Mode); iOS erase all content; when to choose each
App compatibility
side-loaded apps, developer mode, managing app permissions post-update
assess: Troubleshoot flowchart: build a decision tree for diagnosing 'app keeps crashing' on Android
Lab: demonstrate clearing app cache vs. app data on Android and explain the difference
Short answer: a user's iPhone is stuck in a restart loop after an update — what is the first step?

title: Troubleshooting Mobile Security Issues
obj: Students will be able to identify symptoms of mobile security compromises and apply remediation steps.
content: Signs of compromise
unexpected data usage, unfamiliar apps, overheating, pop-up ads, account activity alerts
Unauthorized account access
checking login history, revoking third-party app access, changing passwords, enabling MFA
Malicious apps
identifying apps with excessive permissions or poor reputation; removal steps
MDM compliance issues
device out of compliance alerts, re-enrolling in MDM, selective wipe trigger
Lost or stolen device response
remote lock, remote locate, remote wipe decision; filing a police report; notifying IT
Avoiding reinfection
reviewing installed apps post-clean, changing all credentials, enabling MFA
assess: Scenario response: given a 'device stolen' scenario, list the immediate steps in order
App audit: review a list of 5 apps with their permission sets and flag any that appear malicious
Short answer: what should a user do immediately if they notice unfamiliar purchases on an account linked to their phone?

title: Troubleshooting PC Security Issues
obj: Students will be able to identify symptoms of PC security compromise, perform malware removal, and restore a system to a trusted state.
content: Infection symptoms
slow performance, browser redirects, new toolbars, antivirus disabled, ransom note, encrypted files
Quarantine procedures
isolate from network immediately; document symptoms; preserve evidence before cleaning
Safe Mode removal
booting to Safe Mode with Networking; running anti-malware offline; removing stubborn malware
Browser hijack removal
resetting browser settings, removing extensions, clearing DNS cache, flushing HOSTS file
Post-removal hardening
patch the entry point, change credentials, enable MFA, verify backup integrity
When to reimage
rootkits, persistent re-infection, ransomware without backup recovery, evidence preservation for forensics
assess: Removal walkthrough: given a scenario with specific symptoms, list the complete removal procedure step by step
Lab: use Windows Defender offline scan on a VM and document findings
Short answer: why is the first step of malware response to isolate the machine from the network?
